normal.threat_summary: Vehicle cybersecurity status is Secure. No anomalous activity detected.

trend.label: Degrading
trend.description: Cyber Health is 81/100 and degrading over the last 10 windows. All monitoring dimensions show normal behavior.

root_cause_analysis.root_cause_text: No significant deviations detected. All features within expected operating ranges.

future_risk_forecast.forecast_text: Projected to remain Secure at 82/100 over the next 20 windows.

normal.recommended_response: No action required. Continue passive monitoring.
normal.narrative: Vehicle cybersecurity status is Secure. No anomalous activity detected. Cyber Health is 81/100 and degrading over the last 10 windows. All monitoring dimensions show normal behavior.

metadata.engine: AutoShield Edge Threat Story Engine v1

dos.threat_summary: HIGH RISK: Strong indicators of active cyber attack detected. Vehicle cybersecurity is compromised — immediate attention required.

trend.label: Stable
trend.description: Cyber Health dropped to 22/100 and is stable over the last 10 windows. Primary concern: Threat component (0% of normal).

root_cause_analysis.root_cause_text: Attack signature detected. Primary driver: Threat — overall anomaly signal at -11.3 sigma from normal (below normal). Secondary feature: overall anomaly signal at -11.3 sigma.

future_risk_forecast.forecast_text: Critical escalation risk. Health projected at 66/100 in 20 windows — may cross into Critical range.

dos.recommended_response: ACTIVATE COUNTERMEASURES: Isolate suspected ECUs. Enable rate-limiting on CAN bus. Alert fleet operator. Log forensic data for post-incident analysis.

attack_info.mechanism: CAN bus flooding with high-priority messages
attack_info.signature: elevated message rate with reduced CAN ID diversity
attack_info.impact: bandwidth exhaustion, ECU denial of service
attack_info.mitigation: rate-limiting gateway filter, suspect ECU isolation

dos.narrative: HIGH RISK: Strong indicators of active cyber attack detected. Vehicle cybersecurity is compromised — immediate attention required. Cyber Health dropped to 22/100 and is stable over the last 10 windows. Primary concern: Threat component (0% of normal). Pattern matches Denial of Service attack: elevated message rate with reduced CAN ID diversity. Impact: bandwidth exhaustion, ECU denial of service. Root cause: Attack signature detected. Primary driver: Threat — overall anomaly signal at -11.3 sigma from normal (below normal). Secondary feature: overall anomaly signal at -11.3 sigma. Critical escalation risk. Health projected at 66/100 in 20 windows — may cross into Critical range. Recommended: ACTIVATE COUNTERMEASURES: Isolate suspected ECUs. Enable rate-limiting on CAN bus. Alert fleet operator. Log forensic data for post-incident analysis.

metadata.engine: AutoShield Edge Threat Story Engine v1

fuzzy.threat_summary: CRITICAL: Confirmed cyber attack in progress. Vehicle cybersecurity health is at a dangerous level.

trend.label: Degrading
trend.description: Cyber Health is 13/100 — Critical level. Health has degrading by 3 points over the last 10 windows.

root_cause_analysis.root_cause_text: Confirmed attack pattern. Primary driver: Threat — overall anomaly signal at -11.3 sigma from normal (below normal). Multiple features outside safe bounds: CAN ID diversity, CAN identifier entropy, message burst activity, timing irregularity, payload variation.

future_risk_forecast.forecast_text: Health projected to remain at 65/100 or lower. Immediate intervention required to prevent system compromise.

fuzzy.recommended_response: EMERGENCY RESPONSE: Isolate vehicle from network. Engage fail-safe mode on all ECUs. Initiate forensic data capture. Alert security operations center.

attack_info.mechanism: Randomized CAN ID and payload injection
attack_info.signature: abnormal CAN ID diversity and entropy
attack_info.impact: unpredictable ECU behavior, potential system crash
attack_info.mitigation: CAN ID whitelist, payload validation gateway

fuzzy.narrative: CRITICAL: Confirmed cyber attack in progress. Vehicle cybersecurity health is at a dangerous level. Cyber Health is 13/100 — Critical level. Health has degrading by 3 points over the last 10 windows. Pattern matches Fuzzy attack: abnormal CAN ID diversity and entropy. Impact: unpredictable ECU behavior, potential system crash. Root cause: Confirmed attack pattern. Primary driver: Threat — overall anomaly signal at -11.3 sigma from normal (below normal). Multiple features outside safe bounds: CAN ID diversity, CAN identifier entropy, message burst activity, timing irregularity, payload variation. Health projected to remain at 65/100 or lower. Immediate intervention required to prevent system compromise. Recommended: EMERGENCY RESPONSE: Isolate vehicle from network. Engage fail-safe mode on all ECUs. Initiate forensic data capture. Alert security operations center.
